▲ Page top
stacks
QuoteReply
# Dec 10 2003 at 12:10 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593362766270
20 posts
Can haste items stack with other haste itmes for higher haste or no? just a silly lille question i want to know about.
▲ Page top
RE: stacks
QuoteReply
# Dec 15 2003 at 10:50 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591794873245
37 posts
no, items cannot stack for haste. the haste item with the greatest haste% takes precedence over any other haste items. stacking only works by stacking a single item, spell, and bard song, and the haste cap changes for each level. Look at this site for more info:
Compendium of Haste Data

Edited, Mon Dec 15 22:56:10 2003
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Nov 16 2003 at 7:58 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591769936913
39 posts

RE: level ?
QuoteReply
# Dec 15 2003 at 10:54 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591794873245
37 posts
I am not sure. I transfered it from my 38 warrior to my 12 pally, and i thought i noticed an increase in attack rate, but i cant be sure. I guess the only way to tell is to keep it on him till i get him to lvl 20 and see if my attack rate increases.
▲ Page top
Is the haste worth losing a slot?
QuoteReply
# Aug 05 2003 at 1:03 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591789378388
Scholar
28 posts
This may sound like a silly question to some, but is the increase in attack worth the ac, stats and resists you have to sacrifice to use this?
I now have a Chitinous Belt (AC: 13 Str: +5 Sta: +5 Wis: +5 Disease Resist: +10 Weight: 1). It just seems like a lot to give up for a little faster attack.

▲ Page top
RE: Is the haste worth losing a slot?
QuoteReply
# Sep 21 2003 at 11:43 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591783965906
235 posts
Stats are very nice to have, but so is more attacks. Having haste results in more attacks, which includes bash (if you use a 1 hander and shield). More attacks results in more damage, which means better aggro hold, and while I dont have an exact formula, I'm sure it results in at least 200 more damage by the end of a fight, and when you kill SO many monsters for an extra 200 damage each mob, it adds up to a LOT; I'd rather do that much extra than have a bit more ac str sta wis and resists. Then again, I'd rather get SCHW and use the chitinous belt too! =)
▲ Page top
RE: sick of BS posts
QuoteReply
# Jan 13 2004 at 2:16 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591795128798
Scholar
60 posts
Correct. Haste is not a reduction in delay, but an increase in attack rate. To find the percentage of delay reduction, use (standard attack rate)/(hasted attack rate). Your standard attack rate is 100%. With the 21% on FBSS, your hasted attack rate is 121%. 100/121 = .8264. Your weapon's effective delay would be 82.64% of whatever its listed delay is. 21% haste = 17.36% lower delay.
▲ Page top
haste info
QuoteReply
# Jul 20 2003 at 5:32 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592257828077
12 posts
Okay real fast, for you those of you that are slightly confused, or those that just want some more defined information on haste, haste %'s, item's with haste, stacking infromation, and haste spells and songs go to this website. It is by far the most comprhensive site on the subject that I have ever seen. It also includes information on how to find your new delay of a weapon after being hasted

Here's That Link:
http://cloud.prohosting.com/~talone/HasteData.htm
▲ Page top
RE: Haste explained yet again
QuoteReply
# Mar 05 2003 at 8:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592256912300
52 posts
Allright, correct me if I'm wrong, but isn't increasing the ammount of weapon swings in a certain ammount of time the same as decreasing the delay of the weapon itself? For example, if I have a Lamentation with 19 delay, and I have Haste on me which makes the Lamentation swing more times per minute, in essence, doesn't that just mean that I have lowered the sword's delay? So while the Haste may not actually change your sword into a 9/15.7 weapon, that is the actual damage/delay ratio of your Lamentation while using the Haste providing by the FBSS. So...if you think damage is affected by Haste, you're correct, it is...but only because damage is affected by delay.
▲ Page top
RE: Haste explained yet again
QuoteReply
# Mar 06 2003 at 6:14 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591765923498
Guru
170 posts
Yes, it's the same, but again people miscalculate haste by assuming 21% means a direct 21% decrease of delay which is completely wrong. For some strange reason there are some people out there who lack fundamental math basics to understand this.

50% Haste on a 20 Delay = 10 Delay = Completely Wrong

50% Haste on a 20 Delay = 13.3 Delay = Correct Haste
▲ Page top
Haste explained yet again
QuoteReply
# Mar 04 2003 at 11:15 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591765923498
Guru
170 posts
Haste has NO direct effect on delay. Haste increases your number of swings, and as a result lowers your delay accordingly. The formula to figure the new delay is: (Weapon Delay)*(100/(100+Haste)) or (Weapon Delay)/(1 + . Haste %)

So for the Lamentation example, the increase number of attacks with the FBSS is equivalent to a 15.7 with no haste. (19)*(100/100+21)) or (19)/(1.21)

EDIT: 15.7 as in delay if you can't fingure it out.

Edited, Tue Mar 4 22:44:28 2003
▲ Page top
Frenzy Spawn Nerfed?
QuoteReply
# Oct 08 2002 at 4:54 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1593965997183
Scholar
76 posts
I'm just curious, did VI nerfed the Frenzy spawn rate by chance? In the past when I camped the Frenzy room, the Frenzy would spawn at least once or twice an hour. Granted that it may not always drop the FBSS, the Frenzy still spawned pretty frequently.

But recently, I've noticed that Frenzy spawn rate has gotten less frequent. For example, in one night, I camped the room for 5 hours straight and the Frenzy didn't spawn once. The next day, I camped it for 4 hours and the Frenzy spawned only twice with one of them having the FBSS. Are the rest of you finding this to be the same on your servers?
▲ Page top
